name: Document UUID
description: A globally unique identifier assigned to a specific medical document instance when the document is created, scanned, uploaded, or ingested into a healthcare information system. This identifier enables reliable document tracking and traceability across distributed healthcare ecosystems including hospital information systems (HIS), electronic health record systems (EHR/EMR), laboratory systems (LIS), insurance claim platforms, pharmacy systems, regulatory reporting systems, and interoperability networks. The UUID ensures that every medical document can be uniquely referenced for auditing, data exchange, legal verification, and long-term archival purposes.
guideline: The identifier must follow RFC 4122 UUID Version 4 format. It must be automatically generated by the system and must never be manually entered, edited, reused, or reassigned. Once created, the UUID must remain immutable throughout the entire lifecycle of the document including updates, transfers, or archival storage. The UUID must remain globally unique across all healthcare entities, systems, and document repositories to ensure reliable traceability, regulatory audit compliance, and prevention of duplicate document identification.

name: Medical Document Type
description: Defines the clinical classification of the healthcare document based on its intended medical, administrative, or regulatory purpose. This classification allows healthcare systems, document processing pipelines, and interoperability platforms to correctly interpret, categorize, store, and exchange the document according to standardized clinical workflows. Document type classification is essential for medical record organization, automated document routing, insurance processing, and healthcare data analytics.
guideline: The value must be selected from a controlled medical document vocabulary to maintain consistency across healthcare systems. Acceptable values may include Prescription, Lab Report, Radiology Report, Discharge Summary, Medical Certificate, Clinical Note, Vaccination Record, Referral Letter, Surgery Report, or Consultation Report. Avoid using arbitrary free-text descriptions or non-standard labels. If the organization maintains an internal document classification taxonomy, it must map to internationally recognized healthcare document categories.

name: Document Date
description: Represents the official date on which the medical document was created, finalized, or issued by the healthcare provider. This date indicates when the clinical or administrative information contained in the document became valid and authoritative for medical, regulatory, or legal purposes. The document date is critical for clinical timelines, patient history tracking, insurance claim validation, medical audits, and compliance reporting.
guideline: The date must follow the ISO 8601 international date format (YYYY-MM-DD). The recorded value must exactly match the date visible on the medical document or the system-generated date when the document was finalized by the healthcare provider. Timezone information may be stored separately if required by the system architecture.

name: Country of Issue
description: Identifies the country in which the healthcare document was officially issued by the medical institution or healthcare provider. This information determines the applicable healthcare regulations, medical licensing frameworks, privacy laws, reimbursement standards, and data exchange policies governing the document.
guideline: The country must be represented using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes. Examples include US for United States, IN for India, GB for United Kingdom, DE for Germany, AE for United Arab Emirates, and CN for China. Full country names should not be used to ensure consistent international standardization.

name: Document Language
description: Specifies the primary human language used within the medical document. Healthcare documents may be authored in different languages depending on the country of issuance, institutional practices, or patient demographics. Identifying the document language supports multilingual document processing, translation services, clinical interpretation, and AI-based extraction systems.
guideline: The language must follow ISO 639-1 language codes. Examples include en for English, hi for Hindi, zh for Chinese, fr for French, ar for Arabic, and es for Spanish. If a document contains multiple languages, the primary language used for clinical content should be selected.

name: Hospital Details
description: Contains structured information identifying the healthcare institution responsible for issuing the medical document. These details establish the institutional source of the clinical record and allow healthcare systems, regulatory bodies, insurance providers, and interoperability networks to verify the origin and authenticity of the document.
guideline: All hospital information must correspond to officially registered healthcare facility records as maintained by national healthcare authorities, hospital accreditation organizations, or regulatory registries.

subFields.name: Hospital Name
subFields.description: The legally registered name of the healthcare institution that issued the medical document.
subFields.guideline: The value must match the official hospital registration name used in government healthcare registries or accreditation systems.

subFields.name: Hospital Identifier
subFields.description: A unique identifier assigned to the healthcare institution within a national healthcare registry, hospital network, or internal healthcare system.
subFields.guideline: This identifier should correspond to nationally recognized healthcare facility registration numbers, accreditation identifiers, or institutional codes used within the healthcare ecosystem.

subFields.name: Hospital Address
subFields.description: The official physical address of the healthcare institution issuing the medical document.
subFields.guideline: The address should include building name or number, street information, city, administrative region or state, postal code, and country.

subFields.name: Hospital Contact Number
subFields.description: The primary telephone contact number of the healthcare institution.
subFields.guideline: The number should follow international telephone format including the country code when available.

name: Patient Details
description: Contains personally identifiable information about the patient receiving healthcare services documented in the medical record. This information enables accurate patient identification and ensures that the medical document is associated with the correct individual across healthcare systems.
guideline: All patient information must match official hospital registration records or nationally recognized patient identification systems while complying with healthcare privacy regulations.

subFields.name: Patient Identifier
subFields.description: A unique identifier assigned to the patient within a healthcare system.
subFields.guideline: Examples include Medical Record Number (MRN), ABHA ID in India, NHS Number in the United Kingdom, or other national patient identifiers.

subFields.name: Patient Name
subFields.description: The full legal name of the patient receiving medical care.
subFields.guideline: The value must match the patient name recorded during hospital registration or national healthcare identity records.

subFields.name: Date of Birth
subFields.description: The patient's official date of birth used for patient identification and clinical records.
subFields.guideline: The date must follow ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

subFields.name: Gender
subFields.description: Represents the patient's recorded gender information used in medical documentation.
subFields.guideline: Recommended standardized values include Male, Female, Other, or Unknown.

subFields.name: Patient Address
subFields.description: The residential address of the patient.
subFields.guideline: The address should include city, region or state, postal code, and country.

subFields.name: Patient Contact Number
subFields.description: Primary contact telephone number for the patient.
subFields.guideline: The phone number should follow international phone number formatting including country code when available.

name: Doctor Details
description: Contains identifying information about the licensed medical practitioner responsible for diagnosing, treating, or documenting the patient's medical condition.
guideline: Doctor information must match official medical licensing records maintained by national medical boards or healthcare regulatory authorities.

subFields.name: Doctor Name
subFields.description: The full name of the licensed physician responsible for the patient's care.
subFields.guideline: The name must match the physician's registered professional identity in medical licensing records.

subFields.name: Doctor License Number
subFields.description: Professional license identifier assigned to the physician by a national medical authority.
subFields.guideline: Examples include NPI in the United States, NMC registration in India, or GMC registration in the United Kingdom.

subFields.name: Doctor Specialization
subFields.description: The medical specialty practiced by the physician responsible for treatment.
subFields.guideline: Examples include Cardiology, Dermatology, Pediatrics, Orthopedics, Neurology, or Internal Medicine.

name: Medical Record Details
description: Represents the clinical section of the document where physicians record the patient's symptoms, diagnostic findings, medical diagnosis, and recommended treatment plan.
guideline: All entries must accurately reflect the physician's clinical assessment and should align with internationally recognized medical terminology standards whenever possible.

subFields.name: Symptoms
subFields.description: Symptoms reported by the patient or observed by the physician during medical evaluation.
subFields.guideline: Symptoms should be described using medically meaningful terminology.

subFields.name: Diagnosis
subFields.description: The physician's clinical determination of the patient's medical condition.
subFields.guideline: Diagnosis descriptions should align with recognized medical terminology systems.

subFields.name: Diagnosis Code
subFields.description: A standardized clinical classification code representing the diagnosed medical condition.
subFields.guideline: Diagnosis codes should follow international clinical coding systems such as ICD-10, ICD-11, or SNOMED CT.

subFields.name: Treatment Plan
subFields.description: Describes the recommended medical treatment strategy prescribed by the physician.
subFields.guideline: Treatment plans may include medication prescriptions, therapy recommendations, surgical procedures, lifestyle advice, or scheduled follow-up visits.

name: Prescription Details
description: Represents medications prescribed by the physician as part of the patient's treatment plan.
guideline: Each entry must represent one medication prescribed to the patient.

subFields.name: Medicine Name
subFields.description: The name of the medication prescribed to the patient.
subFields.guideline: Generic drug names should be used whenever possible to ensure consistency across healthcare systems.

subFields.name: Dosage
subFields.description: The amount of medication administered per intake.
subFields.guideline: Examples include 500 mg, 250 mg, or 1 tablet.

subFields.name: Frequency
subFields.description: Indicates how often the medication should be taken.
subFields.guideline: Examples include Once daily, Twice daily, Every 8 hours, or Before meals.

subFields.name: Duration
subFields.description: The total period for which the medication should be taken.
subFields.guideline: Examples include 5 days, 10 days, or 2 weeks.

name: Laboratory Results
description: Represents diagnostic laboratory test results associated with the patient's medical evaluation.
guideline: Each entry must represent a single laboratory test performed for the patient.

subFields.name: Test Name
subFields.description: Name of the diagnostic laboratory test performed.
subFields.guideline: Examples include Blood Glucose Test, Hemoglobin Test, Cholesterol Test, or COVID-19 PCR Test.

subFields.name: Test Code
subFields.description: Standardized identifier used to represent the laboratory test.
subFields.guideline: Whenever possible, the test code should follow LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) standards.

subFields.name: Result Value
subFields.description: The measured outcome of the laboratory test.
subFields.guideline: Results may be numerical values or qualitative indicators such as Positive, Negative, or Normal.

subFields.name: Unit
subFields.description: Measurement unit associated with the test result.
subFields.guideline: Examples include mg/dL, mmol/L, or g/dL.

subFields.name: Reference Range
subFields.description: Normal reference interval used to interpret the laboratory result.
subFields.guideline: Reference ranges must follow medically accepted laboratory standards.

name: Currency
description: Specifies the currency used for any billing, insurance, or financial transactions associated with the medical document.
guideline: Currency must follow ISO 4217 currency codes such as USD, INR, EUR, or GBP.

name: Medical Compliance Details
description: Contains information related to regulatory compliance, healthcare data protection laws, and interoperability standards associated with the medical document.
guideline: All compliance information must align with recognized healthcare regulatory frameworks and interoperability standards used by healthcare systems.

subFields.name: Data Privacy Regulation
subFields.description: Specifies the healthcare data protection regulation governing the document.
subFields.guideline: Examples include HIPAA (United States), GDPR (European Union), or other national healthcare data privacy laws.

subFields.name: Healthcare Standard
subFields.description: Identifies the clinical interoperability standard used for healthcare data exchange.
subFields.guideline: Examples include HL7, FHIR, or CDA.

subFields.name: Digital Signature
subFields.description: A cryptographic signature applied to the medical document to verify authenticity and ensure that the document has not been altered after issuance.
subFields.guideline: Digital signatures must comply with national digital health certification frameworks and electronic signature regulations.
